rulings.Case Title: ANTONIO C. GILTON ET AL VS. VELOCITY COMMERCIAL CAPITAL, LLC, A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alt, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 1, 2024 line 1. DEFENDANT VELOCITY COMMERCIAL CAPITAL, LLC, U.S. BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION, MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alt, Summary Adjudication is continued to November 8, 2024. No later than November 4, 2024 Plaintiff to file a supplemental brief limited to citations to case where a cause of action for Cancellation of Trustee's Deed upon Sale/to Set Aside the Trustee Sale was maintained in the absence of the purchaser/new owner. There shall be no argument. If no case are located, the brief shall be limited to "no cases." =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CYNTHIA BYRD VS. CYRONE BYRD ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Reconsider Motion To Set Aside Entry Of Default And Default Judgment, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE CHIH-LUN YU ET AL VS. MART ALBERT HAITJEMA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 1, 2024 line 3. DEFENDANT MART HAITJEMA, ELAINE CHOW DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED. First Amended Complaint fails to allege facts sufficient to support a cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty as to either Defendant. Plaintiff's attempt to introduce extrinsic evidence at the demurrer stage via a declaration is not supported by any authority. Declaration of Zoe Gallagher is stricken. Whether the Court grants leave to amend or not depends on whether the trial remains on November 4, 2024. If parties obtain a trial continuance in department 206, demurrer will be sustained with leave to amend. If trial remains on November 4, 2024 demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. If trial is continued, Plaintiff may appear ex-parte providing an update regarding the trial date on November 6, 2024 and obtain an order granting leave to amend. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JOSH ANDERSON VS. AMY BEASOM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens And For Statutory Legal Costs Memorandum Of Points And Authorities Request For Judicial Notice; Declaration Of Jonathan Madison, Esq.
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 1, 2024 line 4. DEFENDANT AMY BEASOM Notice Of Motion And Motion To Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens And For Statutory Legal Costs Memorandum Of Points And Authorities Request For Judicial Notice; Declaration Of Jonathan Madison, Esq. is GRANTED. The Complaint on file upon which a Lis Pendens was recorded and maintained for months, does not state a cause of action affecting title or possession. It is a complaint for damages only. Defendant's request for costs and fees is denied. It is not supported by any evidence regarding hours, rates, work performed or costs. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ANDRE WILLIAMS ET AL VS. SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY 1815 EGBERT AVE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 1, 2024 line 5. rulings.Case Title: ANDRE WILLIAMS ET AL VS. SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY 1815 EGBERT AVE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Relcassification Of Case To Civil Rights, Ada Compliance Department
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 1, 2024 line 5. PLAINTIFF ANDRE WILLIAMS, TIMOJENE WATSON Motion For Relcassification Of Case To Civil Rights, Ada Compliance Department is Denied. Insufficient notice per CCP 1005. The Court notes that this action, alleging a claim by a section 8 voucher holder against the Housing Authority regarding housing discrimination and failure to accommodate, properly belongs in the Housing Law and Motion Department. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: EQUITY RESIDENTIAL MANAGEMENT, LLC VS. ANIYAH KEITH ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 1, 2024 line 6. PLAINTIFF EQUITY RESIDENTIAL MANAGEMENT, LLC Motion For Entry Of Judgment is continued to December 4, 2024 for Plaintiff to comply with the Court's October 22, 2024 minute order requesting a supplemental brief. The brief filed on October 25, 2024 did not comply with the Court's order and is stricken. Plaintiff shall have until November 26, 2024 to file and serve a supplemental brief limited to citations to cases where Stipulation for Entry of Judgment (not an actual judgment) was enforced pursuant to CCP 128. CCP 128 is the only code section cited in the "Court Has Authority to Enter Judgment" section of the moving brief. If no such case authority is located, the brief shall state "no cases." No further argument shall be included in the brief. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
